title: Magnificently Slow Cooked Australian Lamb Casserole
description: A peppery, crunchy, and indulgent Australian dish featuring nduja, slow-cooked to perfection in a scallop shell, this lamb casserole is a true delight. It is gluten-free friendly, making it accessible to a wide range of diners.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion, garlic, carrots, and potatoes to the skillet and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the nduja, peppercorns, and salt, and cook for 1 minute.
Transfer the mixture to a scallop shell or a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Pour in the chicken broth and cover the dish with aluminum foil.
Bake for 2.5 hours, then remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 30 minutes.
Remove the dish from the oven and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
Let the casserole rest for 10 minutes before serving.
